description: A module or file defines a class, function, or interface.
A class inherits from a parent class or base class.
A component implements a named design pattern or architectural pattern.
A module or file imports another module or package.
A function or method calls another function or method.
A subclass overrides a method from its parent class.
A training script or process trains a machine learning model on a dataset.
A model or training step computes a loss value using a loss function.
An optimizer updates model parameters to minimize a loss.
Code loads model weights or state from a checkpoint file.
Code saves model weights or state to a checkpoint file.
Code draws samples from a model or probability distribution.
A model generates output tokens, sequences, or predictions.
A component encodes input data into a representation.
A component decodes a representation back to output.
A tokenizer converts text into tokens or token IDs.
An embedding layer maps discrete tokens to dense vector representations.
An attention mechanism attends over a sequence of keys and values.
A normalization layer normalizes activations (e.g., LayerNorm, BatchNorm).
A linear or projection layer transforms a representation to a new space.
A network module executes a forward computation pass.
Autograd or manual gradient computation executes a backward pass.
A model or system achieves a measured performance score on a benchmark dataset.
A model is evaluated on a dataset or benchmark to measure performance.
A configuration or hyperparameter setting is applied to a component.
A test verifies the behavior or output of a component.
A component has a dependency on another component or library.
